data.title: Nymex Overview: Petroleum Futures Continue to Rise on Uncertainty Over Iran Conflict — OPIS
data.description:
Crude oil and refined product futures were again trading higher late Tuesday morning as traders continue to focus on developments in the Middle East.

The ICE Brent contract and Nymex April refined products contracts expire on Tuesday and prices varied widely with most activity focused on next-month contracts.

The May Brent contract was up $6.23 to $11.01/bbl in light trading at about 11:15 a.m. ET, while the more-active June contract was 38cts higher at $107.77/bbl.

West Texas Intermediate crude follows a different trading calendar, so most remains focused on the May contract, which was up $1.35 to $104.23/bbl. The June WTI contract was 60cts higher at $96.62/bbl.

The lightly traded Nymex April ULSD contract was down 3.49cts to $4.3294/gal, and the more-active May ULSD was 1.31cts higher at $4.2188/gal. April RBOB was up 1.6cts to $3.3675/gal and May RBOB was 1.8cts higher at $3.2778/gal.

Crude contracts traded in a wide range, with about $6 separating the morning highs and lows for WTI and June Brent.

Refined product contracts also traded in a wide range, with about 23cts separating the morning highs and lows in the ULSD futures, while May RBOB's morning highs and lows separated by 18cts.

Traders' attention remains fixed on developments in the U.S. and Israel's war against Iran. Petroleum futures retreated after The Wall Street Journal reported President Trump told aides he's willing to end the war even if Iran remains in control of the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Trump, however, has also continued to warn that the U.S. will attack Iranian power plants if the country doesn't reopen the strait and agree to a peace deal.

In the U.S., the conflict on Monday sent the average retail price of gasoline above $4/gal for the first time since the summer of 2022.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

Reporting by Steven Cronin, scronin@opisnet.com; Editing by Jeffrey Barber, jbarber@opisnet.com

data.title: Vertiseit publishes the Annual Report for 2025
data.description:
Vertiseit AB's Annual Report for 2025 is now available on the company's websitevertiseit.com

Links

Link to Vertiseit Investor Relations where the report is available

Contacts

Jonas Lagerqvist, Vertiseit Deputy CEO / CFO / Investor Relations

jonas.lagerqvist@vertiseit.com

+46 732 036 298

Redeye Nordic Growth AB is the company’s Certified Adviser

About Vertiseit

Vertiseit is a leading platform company within In-Store Experience Management (IXM). The company operates through its subidiaries Dise, Grassfish and Visual Art that enable global brands and leading retailers to strengthen the customer experience by offering a seamless customer journey through connecting the physical and digital meeting. The company has around 270 employees in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Austria, Germany, Spain, UK and USA. During the period 2012-2025, Vertiseit performed an average profitable growth of recurring SaaS revenue (ARR) of 48 percent (CAGR). For the full year of 2025, the group’s net revenue amounted to SEK 676 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16 percent. Since 2019, Vertiseit’s B-share is listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market.

data.title: Nike Is Trading at 8 Year Low Ahead of Earnings. Why Analysts Call It a Buy. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kit Norton

Nike fiscal third-quarter earnings are due after the closing bell Tuesday and management will be seeking to convince investors that the company's business turnaround is the real deal.

Wall Street expects the Beaverton, Ore.-based footwear and athletic apparel retailer to post fiscal third-quarter earnings of 29 cents per share, representing a 46.3% drop-off compared with a year ago, with revenue totaling $11.23 billion, which would mark a 0.3% decline year-over-year, according to FactSet.

A team of Jefferies analysts, led by Randal Konik, on Monday wrote they expect fiscal third-quarter performance to be driven by sales in North America and in Nike's wholesale segment. However, the analysts believe the China market and direct-to-consumer sales "remain under pressure."

The firm added that Nike is progressing in turning around its business and that momentum is building in North America.

The third-quarter earnings results come at 4:15 p.m. Eastern time on Tuesday. Company executives are scheduled to hold the conference call at 5 p.m. ET.

During the conference call, investors are expected to be looking to see if the company changes course and decides to provide full-year guidance. Nike has refrained from offering a full-year outlook for several quarters now as it works on its business turnaround.

Recent investor focus has been on Nike leadership and CEO Elliott Hill, who took the job in October 2024, on improving the company's business direction and on maintaining stable growth.

However, the market has been unconvinced thus far by Hill and Nike.

Nike stock advanced 1.7% to $52.10 on Tuesday ahead of the earnings report. For comparison, the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 1.3% and 0.9%, respectively.

On Monday, Nike closed at $50.98, it's lowest closing price since Oct. 13, 2017, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Entering Tuesday, Nike shares were down 17.59% in March, part of a 19.57% decline on the year.

In December 2025, Nike surpassed Wall Street predictions for both fiscal second-quarter earnings and revenue, but investors were disappointed over declining profits and lackluster sales in China.

Nike's third-quarter guidance also did not sit well with investors, with the footwear and athletic apparel retailer forecasting modest growth in North America with trends in Greater China and Converse in line with the second quarter.

Nike also expects gross margins to be down between 1.75 and 2.25 percentage points from a year ago.

"Over the past several quarters, Nike has struggled significantly, amid a confluence of unfavorable external and internal hindrances, which have weighed meaningfully upon sales growth and profit expansion," Oppenheimer analysts Brian Nagel and Andrew Chasanoff wrote Friday.

The analysts added that challenges persist for Nike but the current share price indicates "downbeat market sentiment," which could lead to "favorable re-rating as fundamental trends at the company re-strengthen and start to consistently top Street forecasts."

Oppenheimer has a price target of $120 on Nike, along with a "top pick" designation in its "consumer growth & ecommerce" coverage group. The firm has an Outperform rating on Nike.

It appears Nike's turnaround is far from over. In a March 5 regulatory filing, Nike said it expects to take a pretax charge of about $300 million, mostly in the third quarter, because of employee severance costs over a nine month period ending Feb. 28. The company said the move comes as management continues to evaluate opportunities to operate more efficiently and profitably.

However, the Jefferies analysts wrote that Nike's business is tracking in the right direction and that the upcoming FIFA World Cup is a clear global catalyst.

"We advise buying shares now as the turnaround advances," Konik wrote. Jefferies has a Buy rating on the stock with a $110 price target.

Write to Kit Norton at kit.norton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: KKR Plans to Offer to Acquire Taiyo Holdings
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

KKR intends to make a tender offer to acquire Japanese electronic materials manufacturer Taiyo Holdings for 4,750 Japanese Yen (US$29.88) a share.

The price represents a 117% premium to the six-month average unaffected closing price of Taiyo shares as of May 27, 2025.

Taiyo said its board will support the offer. Taiyo investors DIC and Kowa have also each agreed to sell their shares to Taiyo Holdings through a share consolidation and buyback. Meanwhile, Oasis Management agreed to tender its shares representing about 16% of Taiyo outstanding shares.

With these agreements, KKR has secured support for the deal from shareholders representing about 42% of the company's outstanding shares.

Following the privatization, Taiyo Holdings' founding family plans to re-invest in KJ005HD, the KKR-managed investment vehicle that will own Taiyo Holdings.

"As a private company, we will be able to pursue long-term investments in our core technologies with greater focus and stability, which we could not achieve on our own," Taiyo Chief Executive Hitoshi Saito said.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.title: Australia Probes Meta, Snap, TikTok Over Under-16 Ban, Flags A$49.5M Risk
data.description:
Regulatory pressure on social media platforms could be moving into a more consequential phase, as Australia's online safety watchdog begins its first formal compliance review into how major platforms are enforcing a nationwide ban on users under 16. The probe targets Meta Platforms , Snap , TikTok and YouTube, with the regulator flagging significant concerns around enforcement gaps just months after the law took effect on Dec. 10, 2025. Early findings suggest safeguards may not be robust enough to stop underage users from creating accounts, with some systems allowing repeated attempts at age verification until a user succeeds, raising questions about how effectively these platforms are policing access.

The regulator's initial assessment could carry both financial and reputational implications, with potential penalties reaching as high as A$49.5 million ($34 million) if companies are found not to have taken reasonable steps to comply. Authorities have started gathering evidence and could decide on enforcement actions by mid-year, indicating that this review may evolve into a broader regulatory test case. While officials noted there are fewer under-16 users compared with four months ago, they also indicated that significant numbers of underage users remain active, suggesting compliance measures may still be falling short of expectations.

The timing adds another layer of pressure, coming shortly after Meta and Alphabet Inc. were found liable in a US case tied to a 20-year-old woman's mental health struggles linked to social media use, with damages awarded. That ruling has raised comparisons to a potential Big Tobacco moment for the sector, as legal and regulatory scrutiny appears to be building. Regulators argue platforms have the capability to comply today, while companies such as Meta have said that accurately determining user age remains a challenge across the industry, leaving investors weighing a potentially shifting risk profile if enforcement intensifies.

data.title: Fractile Seeks $200 Million as Nvidia Faces Inference Pressure
data.description:
Fractile, a UK AI chip start-up, is testing investor appetite for alternatives to Nvidia's dominance. The company is in talks to raise more than $200 million at a roughly $1 billion valuation. The round, which hasn't yet been finalized, would include venture capital firm Accel and existing backer Oxford Science Enterprises. It comes after UK rival Olix raised $220 million at a similar valuation, signaling growing investor interest in British inference-focused chip companies. Prior to these talks, Fractile had raised about $15 million in seed financing.

Fractile's architecture uses SRAM co-located with compute to reduce latency and improve efficiency, targeting inference where traditional GPU setups can become constrained. That positioning mirrors a broader industry push, including Groq's approach and Nvidia's own response with new inference-focused chips unveiled at GTC.

The team includes veterans from Nvidia, Imagination Technologies, and Graphcore, the latter acquired for just over $600 million in 2024. Fractile is also leaning into sovereign AI demand, with a 100 million UK expansion plan. If the round closes near target, it would mark a step change in scale and signal that capital is increasingly backing inference as the next battleground.

data.title: Belgium's Fluxys 2025 net profit falls on hydrogen, CO2 business expenditure
data.description:
Overview

  • Belgium energy infrastructure operator's 2025 revenue rose about 7% to EUR 650.5 mln

  • Net profit fell to EUR 74.9 mln, mainly due to hydrogen and CO2 business expenditure

  • Company proposed unchanged EUR 1.40 per share dividend for 2025

Outlook

  • Company says regulated profit/loss will evolve based on equity, financial structure, interest rates, incentives

  • Fluxys Belgium cites uncertainty in natural gas and LNG markets due to geopolitical developments

Result Drivers

  • TARIFF CHANGES - Revenue increase mainly due to changes in components covered by regulated tariffs

  • HYDROGEN AND CO2 EXPENDITURE - Net profit decline primarily due to expenditure for hydrogen and CO2 business, for which the regulatory framework is currently being developed

data.title: SEC Highlights Financial Independence During Financial Literacy Month
data.description:
Washington, D.C.--(Newsfile Corp. - March 31, 2026) - The Securities and Exchange Commission’s Office of Investor Education and Assistance (OIEA) today announced that as part of April’s National Financial Literacy Month it will highlight financial planning tools and resources on Investor.gov to encourage people to build toward financial independence.

Throughout April, SEC staff will provide information to help investors as they create a plan to reach their financial goals and pursue their version of financial independence. Key considerations include starting early; living within your means; investing consistently in a long-term, diversified, risk-appropriate plan; paying down high-interest debt; and having an emergency fund.

"In the spirit of ​America's 250th anniversary, I encourage individuals and families to reflect on the pivotal role that investing can play in achieving their own financial independence," said SEC Chairman Paul S. Atkins. "America's capital markets are the envy of the world, and engaging with them in a risk-appropriate manner can be foundational for a strong financial future."

Staff will also provide investors with information about the benefits of long-term investing using tax-advantaged accounts, building wealth, the power of compound growth, avoiding scams and planning for retirement.

"Investing early helps Americans build wealth for a strong financial future," said John Moses, Acting Director of the SEC's OIEA. “A great way to get started is to create a long-term, diversified plan that helps investors reach their goals. Building wealth slowly by regularly setting money aside for investments helps investors benefit from compound growth, which can drive life-changing results over time.”

OIEA issued an investor bulletin “Investor.gov Tips for 2026” that highlights important information on Investor.gov to help investors make informed investment decisions and avoid investment fraud. Investors can also test their investing knowledge by taking April’s Financial Literacy Month Quiz.

SEC outreach events in April include presentations to military service members and veterans; webinars and events providing investor education and fraud prevention information to older adults; and financial education activities for teachers and students. For more information, visit Investor.gov.

data.title: CoreWeave Closes $8.5B Loan Facility for Cloud Platform Expansion
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

CoreWeave closed a $8.5 billion delayed draw term loan facility to support expanding its artificial intelligence cloud platform.

The deal enables the cloud services provider to borrow up to $7.5 billion initially, with the ability to increase total borrowing capacity to $8.5 billion as underlying assets reach stabilization, the company said Tuesday.

The facility provides CoreWeave with more access to low-cost capital. It fulfills financing requirements to deliver previously contracted cloud services with leading AI enterprises, expanding CoreWeave's high-performance AI cloud footprint.

The facility received an A3 rating by Moody's and an A (low) rating by DBRS.

MUFG and Morgan Stanley were co-structuring agents and joint bookrunners with Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan serving as additional coordinating lead arrangers for the deal. The facility was anchored by Blackstone Credit & Insurance.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com
